prompt: A mortgage loan originator (MLO) primarily:

options.text: Appraises property value independently for the lender only
Takes a residential mortgage application and/or offers/negotiates terms for compensation
Sets the Federal Reserve discount rate

explanation: SAFE Act definitions center on taking applications and offering/negotiating terms for compensation.

prompt: A borrower's debt-to-income (DTI) ratio generally compares:

options.text: Home equity to purchase price only
Monthly debt obligations to gross monthly income
Property taxes to HOA fees only

explanation: DTI = monthly debts ÷ gross monthly income (front-end/back-end variants exist).

prompt: Loan-to-value (LTV) is calculated as:

options.text: Loan amount ÷ lesser of purchase price or appraised value (typical purchase)

explanation: LTV measures loan size relative to property value.

prompt: PITI typically includes:
explanation: PITI is the standard housing payment stack used in underwriting.

prompt: A purchase contract contingency for financing generally allows a buyer to:

options.text: Exit or renegotiate if they cannot obtain specified financing
Force the seller to pay all closing costs by law
Avoid disclosing income to the lender

explanation: Financing contingencies protect buyers if loan approval fails under stated terms.

distractorExplanations.d: Income disclosure is required for underwriting.

prompt: An underwriter's primary job is to:

options.text: Evaluate credit, capacity, collateral, and compliance risk against guidelines
Record the deed as county clerk

explanation: Underwriting assesses whether the loan meets program and risk standards.

prompt: A rate lock typically:

options.text: Protects the quoted interest rate for a stated period if conditions are met
Eliminates the need for an appraisal

explanation: Locks hold a rate/points for a window; closing is not guaranteed by the lock alone.

prompt: Front-end DTI focuses on:

options.text: Housing payment (often PITI) relative to income
All revolving debt only excluding housing

explanation: Front-end (housing) ratio compares housing costs to income.

distractorExplanations.b: That's closer to other debt components of back-end.

prompt: A conventional loan that is not government-insured/guaranteed is typically sold to:

options.text: GSEs like Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac if it meets conforming guidelines (among other investors)
The FDIC as insurer of the note

explanation: Conforming conventional loans often sell to GSEs when within limits/guidelines.

distractorExplanations.d: FDIC insures deposits, not mortgages as GSE buyer.

prompt: Pre-approval vs pre-qualification: pre-approval generally means:

options.text: A casual verbal estimate with no credit check ever
A more verified underwriting review of credit/income/assets (still not a final commitment)

explanation: Pre-approval is stronger than pre-qual but is not the same as a final loan commitment.

prompt: Occupancy type 'primary residence' generally means the borrower:

options.text: Will never live in the property
Intends to occupy the property as their main home
Must rent it on day one

explanation: Primary occupancy is the borrower's principal residence.

prompt: Gift funds for a down payment typically require:

options.text: A gift letter and evidence of transfer/sourcing per program rules
That gifts come only from the listing agent
That gifts be repaid monthly like a loan without disclosure

explanation: Agencies require gift documentation and paper trail; repayment may recharacterize as a loan.

distractorExplanations.c: Donor rules are specific; agents are conflicted.

prompt: A jumbo loan is generally:

options.text: A loan amount above conforming loan limits
A HELOC that never has a credit limit

explanation: Jumbo = above conforming limits (investor overlays apply).

prompt: Credit inquiries for mortgage shopping within a short window are often treated as:

options.text: Always each a separate permanent score hit forever
A single inquiry for scoring purposes under common scoring model rules

explanation: Rate-shopping windows commonly collapse multiple mortgage inquiries.

prompt: Clear-to-close means:

options.text: Underwriting conditions are satisfied and the loan may proceed to closing
The property tax bill is waived forever
The MLO becomes the title insurer

explanation: CTC indicates conditions are met for closing (final walk-through/funding still matter).

prompt: An FHA loan is distinctive because it is:

options.text: Uninsured and only for investment condos
Insured by the Federal Housing Administration, often allowing lower down payments

explanation: FHA provides mortgage insurance enabling flexible credit/down-payment profiles.

prompt: VA loans are generally available to:

options.text: Eligible veterans, service members, and certain surviving spouses

explanation: VA guaranty programs serve eligible military-related borrowers.

prompt: Private mortgage insurance (PMI) on conventional loans is typically required when:
explanation: Conventional PMI commonly applies when equity is below 20%.

prompt: A fixed-rate mortgage keeps:

options.text: The interest rate constant for the life of the loan (payment may still change with escrow)
The escrow taxes forever at zero
A rate that resets monthly by statute

explanation: Fixed-rate locks the note rate; PITI can change if taxes/insurance change.

prompt: An adjustable-rate mortgage (ARM) interest rate:

options.text: Can adjust based on an index plus margin after an initial fixed period
Is set only by the county assessor

explanation: ARMs adjust per note terms: index + margin, caps, etc.

prompt: A USDA rural development loan generally targets:

options.text: Eligible borrowers in qualifying rural/suburban areas meeting income limits
Only commercial farms with no residence

explanation: USDA SFH programs have geography and income eligibility rules.

distractorExplanations.c: Different USDA farm programs exist; SFH is residential.

prompt: Interest-only loans during the IO period typically:

options.text: Require payment of interest only, so principal may not decline
Are identical to biweekly biweekly principal prepay mandates

explanation: IO payments do not reduce principal unless extra principal is paid.

prompt: A HELOC is best described as:

options.text: A closed-end purchase money first mortgage only
A revolving line of credit secured by home equity

explanation: HELOCs are revolving junior (or sometimes first) liens against equity.

prompt: Discount points are typically paid to:
explanation: Points are prepaid interest/fees used to buy down the rate.

prompt: A balloon mortgage features:

options.text: Fully amortizing payments with no large final payment
A large principal balance due at a stated maturity before full amortization
Only monthly interest that never ends by contract

explanation: Balloons leave a lump-sum balance at term.

prompt: Loan-level price adjustments (LLPAs) primarily affect:

options.text: Pricing/cost based on risk factors like FICO, LTV, product type

explanation: LLPAs adjust secondary-market pricing for risk attributes.

prompt: A construction-to-permanent loan is designed to:

options.text: Finance building then convert to a permanent mortgage

explanation: Construction financing transitions into a long-term mortgage.

prompt: Negative amortization occurs when:

options.text: Payments are insufficient to cover accruing interest, so balance rises
The loan is paid off early

explanation: If interest exceeds payment, unpaid interest is added to principal.

prompt: QM (Qualified Mortgage) ability-to-repay rules are most associated with:

options.text: Underwriting standards designed to assess ability to repay under ATR/QM framework

explanation: ATR/QM is a consumer-protection underwriting framework for closed-end consumer mortgages.

prompt: Mortgage credit certificates (where available) generally provide:

options.text: A federal tax credit related to mortgage interest for eligible borrowers
VA funding fee waivers for all civilians

explanation: MCCs can provide a tax credit on a portion of mortgage interest (program-specific).

distractorExplanations.d: VA rules differ; MCC is separate.

prompt: RESPA primarily addresses:

options.text: Settlement costs, servicing disclosures, and prohibitions on kickbacks/unearned fees
Equal credit without regard to race only (that's ECOA/FHA fair lending)

explanation: RESPA (Reg X) covers settlement process and anti-kickback rules.

prompt: TILA/Reg Z is most associated with:

options.text: Truthful disclosure of credit terms/APR and certain advertising/ability-to-repay rules
Title insurance premiums set by HUD

explanation: TILA focuses on cost-of-credit disclosures and related consumer protections.

prompt: TRID (Know Before You Owe) integrates:

options.text: Loan Estimate and Closing Disclosure timing/content rules

explanation: TRID combines TILA/RESPA disclosures into LE/CD.

prompt: ECOA (Reg B) prohibits discrimination based on:

options.text: Protected characteristics such as race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age (with limits), and receipt of public assistance
Whether the borrower uses a Realtor

explanation: ECOA bars credit discrimination on listed prohibited bases.

distractorExplanations.a: Score can be a legitimate factor.

prompt: The Fair Housing Act prohibits discrimination in housing-related transactions based on:

options.text: Race, color, religion, sex, handicap, familial status, national origin (among others as applied)

explanation: FHA fair housing protected classes govern housing discrimination.

prompt: HMDA requires covered institutions to:

options.text: Provide free legal counsel at closing

explanation: HMDA (Reg C) is a data reporting statute for fair lending analysis/transparency.

prompt: The SAFE Act requires MLOs to:

options.text: Be licensed/registered via NMLS as applicable and meet education/testing standards
Work only for GSEs as employees

explanation: SAFE Act created nationwide licensing/registration standards through NMLS.

prompt: GLBA privacy rules require financial institutions to:

options.text: Share all NPI freely without notice
Provide privacy notices and limit sharing of nonpublic personal information per the rule

explanation: GLBA Privacy Rule governs notice and NPI sharing.

prompt: The Red Flags Rule (identity theft) requires many creditors to:

options.text: Have a written identity theft prevention program
Charge higher rates to all seniors

explanation: Red Flags programs detect/respond to identity theft red flags.

distractorExplanations.c: CIP is BSA/AML related and still needed.

prompt: A Loan Estimate must generally be provided:

options.text: Within 3 business days of application (TRID timing)
Never if the loan is conventional

explanation: TRID requires LE within 3 business days of application.

distractorExplanations.c: Applies broadly to covered closed-end consumer mortgages.

prompt: A Closing Disclosure must generally be received by the consumer:

options.text: At least 3 business days before consummation (with limited exceptions)

explanation: CD timing is a core TRID consumer protection.

prompt: Steering a borrower to a loan that pays the MLO more while harming the borrower can violate:

options.text: Best practices only with no legal risk
TILA anti-steering/LO compensation rules and fair dealing standards
Only the Uniform Commercial Code Article 9 exclusively

explanation: LO compensation/anti-steering rules target harmful steering for compensation.

prompt: Appraisals for many higher-priced mortgage loans require:

options.text: Compliance with appraisal independence and HPML appraisal rules where applicable
That the MLO write the appraisal value

explanation: HPML and AIR rules add appraisal/independence protections.

prompt: Flood insurance is generally required when:

options.text: The property is in a Special Flood Hazard Area and the loan is secured by that improved real estate from a regulated lender
The loan is a HELOC under $100 only
The property is never near water by borrower statement alone

explanation: Mandatory flood insurance applies to SFHA properties with designated loans.

prompt: The Bank Secrecy Act/AML framework for mortgage lenders typically includes:

options.text: Customer identification, monitoring, and SAR filing where required
Banning all cash down payments forever

explanation: BSA/AML programs include CIP, monitoring, and SARs.

distractorExplanations.d: Cash can be sourced; not a blanket ban.

prompt: Charging a borrower a fee for which no service is performed is:

options.text: An unearned fee / potential RESPA Section 8 violation
Always allowed if disclosed in tiny print

explanation: RESPA prohibits unearned fees and kickbacks.

prompt: A kickback for referring settlement business is:
explanation: Section 8 bans referral fees/kickbacks for settlement services.

distractorExplanations.c: AfBAs have disclosure/safe harbor rules, not kickback requirements.
distractorExplanations.d: No such de minimis excuse as stated.

prompt: Misrepresenting a loan as having 'no closing costs' when costs are rolled into the rate/price without clarity can be:
explanation: Misleading cost claims create UDAAP and advertising law risk.

prompt: Dual compensation where a loan originator receives both borrower-paid and lender-paid compensation on the same loan is generally:

options.text: The definition of a discount point

explanation: LO Comp Rule restricts dual compensation structures.

prompt: Appraiser independence rules generally prohibit:

options.text: Coercing or influencing appraisers to hit a target value
Providing the purchase contract to the appraiser when allowed

explanation: AIR/TILA independence bans coercion for predetermined values.

prompt: Ethics require an MLO who discovers a material misstatement on an application to:

options.text: Ignore it if the loan will close
Address it; do not submit known false information

explanation: Submitting false information is fraud/ethics violation.

prompt: Discrimination against a borrower because of familial status violates:

options.text: Only the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice exclusively
Only the SAFE Act education hours

explanation: Familial status is a Fair Housing protected class.

prompt: An affiliated business arrangement (AfBA) disclosure is needed when:

options.text: A referral is made to an affiliate settlement provider
The borrower pays cash for a car

explanation: AfBA disclosures inform consumers of affiliate relationships on referrals.

prompt: Bait-and-switch advertising of a teaser rate not actually available is:

options.text: Acceptable if the website looks premium
Unethical and potentially illegal deceptive advertising

explanation: Advertising must be truthful and available as represented.

prompt: Using a borrower's NPI for personal marketing without authorization can violate:
explanation: Nonpublic personal information is protected under GLBA and ethical standards.

prompt: Splitting a fee with an unlicensed person for taking applications can be a problem because:

options.text: Licensing/SAFE Act and compensation rules may be violated
It is required for jumbo loans

explanation: Only properly licensed/registered individuals may engage in MLO activity for compensation.

prompt: Encouraging a borrower to lie about occupancy (occupancy fraud) is:
explanation: False occupancy is material mortgage fraud.

prompt: Providing the same assistance and information to similarly situated applicants regardless of protected class is part of:
explanation: Consistent, non-discriminatory treatment is core fair lending.

prompt: A 'straw buyer' scheme typically involves:

options.text: Someone posing as the buyer to obtain a loan for another party, often fraudulently

explanation: Straw buyers conceal the true purchaser/borrower—classic fraud pattern.

prompt: UDAAP stands for practices that are:

options.text: Unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices

explanation: UDAAP is a core consumer protection standard (Dodd-Frank/CFPB).
